free_text: Charge distribution in low energy fission of actinides
Radiochemistry Division
Recoiling fission products from
electrodeposited source of 244-Cm on a platinum
planchet were collected for 2 h to 400 h on a 75 mm
thick Lexan polycarbonate foil.
The irradiated solutions or Lexan catcher foils were
used for direct gamma-ray spectrometry.
The irradiated solutions or Lexan catcher
foils as the case may be were counted on a 120 cm3
HPGe detector coupled to a 4K channel analyser in
live time mode, to determine the various fission
product activities.
The resolution of the detector system was 1.8 keV at
1332.0 keV and the dead time was always kept less
than 10%.
The photopeak areas of different gamma rays of the
nuclides of present interest were calculated by
subtracting the linear Compton background from their
total peak areas.
From the photopeak areas, the cumulative and
independent yields of fission products relative to
some internal fission product monitors were
determined using decay-growth equations.
For long and short-lived fission products, 92-Sr and
104-Tc were used as fission rate monitors
respectively.
The cumulative yields of fission products in the
thermal neutron-induced fission of 232-U and 238-Pu
as well as in spontaneous fission of 244-Cm were
converted to their fraction yields using the mass
chain yields.
The fractional cumulative yields (FCY) of a few
fission products were determined from the activities
of the daughter products using a parent-daughter
genetic equation.
Decay data used in the present work
Decay data used in the present work
The uncertainties shown on FCY values are
the propagated errors due to errors on cumulative
yields and mass chain yields where as the
uncertainties quoted in the independent yields are
the standard deviations of replicate measurements.
Approved by Dr.H.Naik, RCD, BARC
Paresh Prajapati and S.Mukherjee
